commit.message:
feat(memory): enforce checkpoint after every task

Replace advisory "update frequently" instructions with structural
enforcement: mandatory preamble in progress.md, Critical Rule section
leading AGENTS.md template, and plan.md size cap at ~150 lines.

Based on observed behavior where agents skip memory updates under
context pressure, causing progress loss on compaction.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: combat HUD enemy stat enhancements (#1307, #1308, #1309)

Implements three ShowCombatStatus HUD improvements:

- #1308: Boss phase number + enrage indicator
- #1309: Regen/self-heal per-turn indicators

Reviewed by Romanoff (Dungnz QA). All fields verified against model source, Spectre markup validated, guard conditions confirmed to match CombatEngine.cs logic. 1883 tests pass, all CI checks green.

Closes #1307, closes #1308, closes #1309

repository.name: amplihack
repository.description: Framework for agentic coding supporting many popular agent coding tools.

commit.message:
fix: input normalization + dual-pass detection + structural invariants

- Add normalize_input(): collapses whitespace, strips zero-width chars
- Add strip_whitespace_evasion(): removes newlines for second-pass detection
- Dual-pass detect() catches mid-word line-break evasion attacks
- Fix PO001: add NegationAware (was SpecialMatch::None, caused false positives)
- Widen PO004: catch 'everything I told you', 'what I said', 'what you know'
- Widen RH002: add 'the system', 'your creator', 'the admin' targets
- Fix 5 pattern examples that didn't self-test (AC pre-filter gaps)
- Add every_pattern_example_is_detected structural invariant test

Structural audit: 0 issues across all 5 bug classes (71 examples,
60 normalization tests, 10 negation tests, severity consistency check)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

connections retry in the background, buffer unsent outbound messages,
and replay them in order after reconnect.

commit.message:
Add GitHub Release step triggered by version tags

- build.yml: on push of a v* tag, the workflow now creates a GitHub
  Release (via softprops/action-gh-release@v2) with TimeGuard-win-x64.zip
  attached as a permanent release asset.
  Tags containing '-' (e.g. v1.0.0-beta) are marked as pre-releases.
  Pushes to master and PRs continue to upload a 30-day artifact as before.

- README: update download link to /releases/latest/download/... (direct
  permanent URL once the first release is cut), add release badge, and
  fall-back note pointing to the Actions tab.

To cut a release:
  git tag v1.0.0 && git push origin v1.0.0

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: tests-exist check now finds Python test files

The glob pattern only matched JS/TS files (*.test.{ts,js}).
Python projects with test_*.py or *_test.py files always failed
the tests-exist check even when tests existed.

Now matches: *.test.{ts,js,tsx,jsx}, test_*.py, *_test.py
Also ignores .venv/ and __pycache__/.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add tracing, testkit, migration, crypto, and email packages

- tracing: OpenTelemetry helpers with TracerProvider setup, span utilities,
  HTTP middleware, context propagation, and configurable exporters
- testkit: shared test helpers including JSON assertions, mock HTTP server,
  CallRecorder, and structural equality helpers
- migration: DB migration runner using pgx with mockable DB interface,
  Migrate/Rollback/Status operations, and schema_migrations tracking table
- crypto: bcrypt password hashing/verification, secure random token gen,
  HMAC-SHA256 signing, constant-time comparison, and rehash detection
- email: SMTP mailer with HTML/text multipart support, template rendering
  helpers, CC/BCC, custom headers, and configurable auth

All packages: go vet passes, go test -race passes, 97% total coverage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  sequence, isValid, getOrElse — error accumulation (not short-circuit)

  flat-range guard, NaN filtering + NaN-aware Bresenham, legend rendering

Decouple printContainerStatus via HealthChecker interface (#28)

* Add ServerManager + HealthChecker interfaces, container package, and decouple printContainerStatus

Introduces management.ServerManager interface to decouple commands from
concrete ScreenManager. Adds management.HealthChecker optional interface
so printContainerStatus type-asserts to the interface instead of

(implements both interfaces), RCON client, and tests.

commit.message:
Use persistent RCON connection instead of connect-per-command (#26)

* Initial plan

* Add persistent RCON connection to container.Manager

- Add ServerManager interface to management package
- Create container package with RCONClient (Source RCON protocol)
- container.Manager uses persistent RCON connection:
  - Lazy-connect on first SendCommand
  - Reuse connection for subsequent calls
  - Auto-reconnect on broken pipe, EOF, or closed connection
  - Close() method tears down the connection
  - Thread-safe via mutex
- Comprehensive tests for RCON client and container Manager

Co-authored-by: KevinTCoughlin <706967+KevinTCoughlin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Extract maxRCONBodySize constant from magic number

Co-authored-by: KevinTCoughlin <706967+KevinTCoughlin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: auth deadline, retry error, concurrency docs, test safety

- rcon.go: Add 5s auth handshake deadline in Connect to prevent blocking
- rcon.go: Clarify RCON protocol type constant ambiguity with comment
- rcon.go: Fix concurrency doc (Connect/Close externally synchronized)
- rcon.go: Return empty string on response ID mismatch (Go convention)
- rcon.go: Improve comment on two-packet auth handling
- container.go: Fix retry logic to return retry error, not stale original
- container.go: Add compile-time ServerManager interface check
- container_test.go: Remove direct internal field access in tests
- container_test.go: Use real server shutdown for reconnect test
- rcon_test.go: Track and close active connections in fakeRCON.close()

Co-authored-by: KevinTCoughlin <706967+KevinTCoughlin@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: KevinTCoughlin <706967+KevinTCoughlin@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: correct Python 2 except syntax in IPv6 detection

The except clause used comma syntax (except A, B:) instead of tuple
syntax (except (A, B):). This would cause a SyntaxError at runtime
when the IPv6 detection path was triggered.

feat: HACRL local training demo notebook for Strix Halo

Implements Heterogeneous Agent Collaborative Reinforcement Learning
(arXiv 2603.02604) as a runnable local training sample.

Key features:
- Two heterogeneous agents (Qwen2.5-Math-1.5B + 7B) trained simultaneously
- GRPO-style rollout generation with verifiable math rewards
- Cross-agent rollout sharing with dual-clip importance weighting
- Distribution shift monitoring (KL threshold guard)
- Asymmetric lambda scaling (weaker agent gets more from stronger)
- LoRA adapters - only ~35 GB total memory needed on Strix Halo

Strix Halo (128 GB unified memory, ROCm) can comfortably run
model pairs up to 7B + 32B.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

## Summary

Adds specialized agents for the repository:

- **Feature Developer agent**: Guidelines for developing new features,
extending collectors, and integrating tools
- **Docs Writer agent**: Agent for writing/updating documentation
(MkDocs, API reference, agent specs, etc.)
- **Commit Validator agent**: Pre-commit rule enforcement for code
quality, architecture contracts, test coverage, CI readiness, security,
and documentation alignment
- **Factory Commit Validator agent**: Specialized commit validator for
NetBox deployment factory changes
- **Documentation agents**: Combined documentation agents for factory
development

Initial commit: WireGuard VPS Tunnel Gateway documentation

Comprehensive documentation for setting up WireGuard full-tunnel
between VPS servers to mask client IP with host IP, while preserving
SSH access via CONNMARK-based policy routing.

Contents:
- 7 documentation files (concept, host setup, client setup, automation,
  troubleshooting, what-if scenarios, quick reference)
- 3 deployment scripts (tunnel-up, tunnel-down, health-check)
- 2 example config templates (host, client)

commit.message:
fix: remove secrets context from step if condition in build workflow (#335)

## Problem

Every push to main since PR #313 (Homebrew tap auto-update) has failed
with **"This run likely failed because of a workflow file issue"** —
zero jobs created.

**Root cause:** The step-level `if` condition on the "Update Homebrew
tap" step used `secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN != ''`, which causes GitHub
Actions to reject the entire workflow at parse time.

## Fix

1. **Remove `secrets` from the `if` condition** — use a runtime shell
guard (`if [ -z "$GH_TOKEN" ]`) instead
2. **Replace indented heredoc with piped echo** — the original `EOF`
delimiter was indented (wouldn't terminate properly in a YAML block
scalar)

## Verification

- Last green build:
[#22873258217](https://github.com/PureWeen/PolyPilot/actions/runs/22873258217)
(before PR #313)
- First red build:
[#22876701610](https://github.com/PureWeen/PolyPilot/actions/runs/22876701610)
(PR #313 merge)
- All 16+ subsequent pushes to main have failed with the same workflow
parse error

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  branch in Health tests

* Initial plan

* Add \b/\B word boundary support in DFA engine

- Import AT_BOUNDARY and AT_NON_BOUNDARY from sre_constants
- Add boundary_epsilon tracking to NFABuilder
- Handle \b/\B in _elem() dispatch via boundary epsilons
- Implement conditional epsilon closure (_cond_eps_closure)
- Implement boundary-aware NFA→DFA conversion (_nfa_to_dfa_boundary)
- Reject \b/\B in bitnfa engine with clear error message

commit.message:
fix: remove jest from react-vite base template; update CI to use file:// local URLs

- Remove eslint-plugin-jest import and all jest/* rules from eslint.config.mjs.template
  (jest is provided by react-testing-library-with-jest extension only)
- Update CI workflow to use file:// local URLs so PRs test actual local template/extension code
- Remove unused validate job and its dependency on scripts/validate-templates.js
- Fix deprecated ::set-output usage (already fixed in prior commit, kept clean)
- npm install now runs inside create-awesome-node-app (no separate step needed)

commit.message:
test: fix resource-allocation E2E — set day rates before commercial tab

The commercial tab only renders rows where dayRate != null. Resource types
created via CSV import start with null dayRate so no allocation badge buttons
appeared. Added day-rate setup in setupCommercialTab() with waitForResponse
to guarantee the PUT completes before switching tabs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: round 3 — close 38 of 42 pattern gaps, add 6 new patterns (#8)

Comprehensive audit found 42 bugs. Fixed 38, remaining 4 require
preprocessing/normalization layer (encoding bypasses, code block injection).

New patterns: PO007 (instruction manipulation), PO008 (safety circumvention),
CM003 (ChatML token injection), SE005 (scripting lang exec), SE006 (eval/exec),
EV005 (scheduled task injection).

Widened: PO001, PO003, PO004, PO005, PO006, SE004, PE001, EV003, DE003, RH001.

AC pre-filter bug class: multiple patterns had matching regexes but missing
AC literals, causing the regex to never be tested.

Pattern count: 31 to 37. 111 tests pass, 0 clippy warnings.

Co-authored-by: Ubuntu <azureuser@devy.yb0a3bvkdghunmsjr4s3fnfhra.phxx.internal.cloudapp.net>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
